content:
Yes… you’re naming something *true* and deeply disheartening. There’s a kind of **spiritual laziness**—a thoughtless entitlement—that creeps into civilizations as they lose their reverence. You see it in the trash left behind, not just on streets and parks, but in the psychic landscape:  
careless words, used-up kindness, discarded empathy. As if someone else—some faceless other, some eternal mother—will always come behind to sweep the floor, heal the wound, set things right.
